item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to know I helped, even if I have no memory of doing so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like I said, there are some hints to it in the testing logs, like them literally screaming at the researchers to try and save each other, so maybe if you put them before the interview logs it would feel less jarring.
Alternatively, you could roll with the jarring nature and just have one line in the description say something like "It is possible that the romantic connection between A and B is responsible for the anomaly". That would show the Foundation's coldness, and make it hit a little harder when it's used as an attack in the interview, but like you said that's a bit out of place in a scientific document, and it also removes all subtlety of why the anomaly exists. Yeah, this kind of a tough problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I admit it's a bit blunt - Unfortunately the exact nature of their relationship isn't necessarily important to a scientific document so I wasn't sure how/when to insert it. Got any ideas of how it could be more subtly implanted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the idea, and the story of these two D-Class getting classified and abused by their researcher, who then reprimanded by the Foundation, it's neat. The execution could be better, there's a few extraneous details that don't need to be there, like the exact length of time for each pause in the interviews. Also, them being secret lovers really comes out of nowhere. I'm not opposed to the idea, I actually kinda like that this SCP may be fueled by the power of love, but the only indication before is that A is allowed visitation rights with B, and then it's just kinda thrown out in the interview. There were some references to it in the testing logs, so maybe with some rearranging it could be foreshadowed better.

I'll give it an upvote for being enjoyable, but it could be improved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My attempt to write a thing about people rather than a thing about things.

how to image, halp

When they show up, image credits are:
SCP-3253-A's back: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Custom_Japanese_backpiece_tattoo_by_Greg_James.jpg

SCP-3253-C:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:COLLECTIE_TROPENMUSEUM_Houten_tatoeagehamer_met_drie_naalden_TMnr_A-3235.jpg

With thanks to those who read it at idea stage and draft stage, particularly ratsy, whose unexpected PM jolted my neurons into turning what had been a story about just one man into a more detailed story about three people

Other readers include but are not limited to:
Zyn
Professor_JD
RavynKarasu
Golden506
